audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in ldapwhoami.c are guarded. `ld` is always non-NULL after `tool_conn_setup(0,0)` because `ldap_initialize` failure calls `exit()`; `res` is non-NULL at `ldap_parse_result` because the loop only breaks when `ldap_result` returns >0; `prog` is set at L120 before any `usage()` call; `matcheddn`/`text` are guarded by `ptr && *ptr` before use. The only implicit assumption is the LDAP library's berval invariant (`bv_len>0 → bv_val!=NULL`), which the OpenLDAP BER decoder maintains.
